name: The Mystery Unveiled
description: An investigation reveals hidden truths, progressively reshaping understanding of events and characters.

nodes.label: Surface Order
nodes.definition: The world appears to be in a stable state, but something is hidden beneath the surface — a crime, a lie, a secret, or a concealed truth that will drive the story.
entry_conditions: The audience sees a world that will soon be disrupted by a question
exit_conditions: An event reveals that something is wrong, hidden, or unexplained
typical_variants: Apparently normal community with a dark secret
Crime scene that presents a puzzle
A death or disappearance that doesn't add up
An anomaly that demands explanation
failure_modes: Surface order is so dull the audience doesn't engage
The hidden truth is so obvious from the start that there's no mystery
signals_in_text: An event that raises a question
Someone who notices what others don't

nodes.label: Central Question Posed
nodes.definition: The mystery is formalized — a specific question demands an answer. Who did it? What happened? What is being hidden? The investigator (or protagonist) commits to finding out.
entry_conditions: A question crystallizes that cannot be ignored
typical_variants: A murder to solve
A disappearance to explain
A conspiracy to uncover
A personal secret that must be understood
failure_modes: Question is too vague to drive investigation

nodes.label: Initial Clues
nodes.definition: The investigator gathers the first evidence — witness statements, physical clues, patterns, or anomalies. An initial theory forms, but it is incomplete or misleading.
exit_conditions: The investigator has a working theory that will later prove incomplete
typical_variants: Physical evidence at the scene
Witness testimony with contradictions
Documentary evidence that raises more questions
A pattern that suggests a suspect
failure_modes: Clues are so clear the mystery is already solved
Clues are so opaque no theory is possible

nodes.label: False Trail
nodes.definition: The investigation pursues a lead that appears promising but proves misleading. The false trail tests the investigator's judgment and delays the truth.
entry_conditions: Initial clues have suggested a direction
The investigator commits resources to pursuing it
exit_conditions: The false trail is exposed and the investigation must reorient
typical_variants: Wrong suspect identified
Planted evidence followed
A confession that turns out to be false
A theory that explains some facts but not all
failure_modes: False trail is obviously wrong from the start
False trail consumes so much time the story stalls
No consequences for following the wrong path
signals_in_text: Pursuing a suspect with apparent evidence
A fact that doesn't fit the theory

nodes.label: Deepening Complexity
nodes.definition: New evidence complicates the picture. The mystery is larger, darker, or more personal than initially assumed. The investigator discovers that the truth threatens powerful interests or their own assumptions.
entry_conditions: The false trail has been abandoned
exit_conditions: The investigator understands the true scope and danger of the mystery
typical_variants: More victims or instances discovered
Conspiracy extends to trusted figures
The investigator's own past or relationships are entangled
The question shifts from 'who' to 'why'
failure_modes: Deepening is just more clues without changing the nature of the mystery
The conspiracy becomes so large it's implausible

nodes.label: Key Breakthrough
nodes.definition: A critical piece of evidence or insight connects the disparate clues into a coherent picture. The truth becomes visible — or nearly visible — for the first time.
entry_conditions: A new clue or perspective provides the missing link
exit_conditions: The investigator knows (or strongly suspects) the truth and must act on it
typical_variants: A witness comes forward
Physical evidence is re-examined with new eyes
The investigator realizes what they overlooked
A pattern becomes visible that was hidden in plain sight
failure_modes: Breakthrough is a convenient coincidence rather than earned
Breakthrough solves everything at once with no remaining uncertainty
signals_in_text: The investigator's demeanor changes — they know

nodes.label: Confrontation with Truth
nodes.definition: The investigator confronts the responsible party or forces the truth into the open. This is dangerous — the truth threatens someone with power, and the investigator may pay a price for revealing it.
entry_conditions: The breakthrough has identified the truth
The investigator must act to expose or resolve it
exit_conditions: The truth is publicly revealed or privately confirmed; the mystery is solved
typical_variants: Direct accusation in front of witnesses
Trap set to provoke a confession
Physical confrontation with the culprit
Public revelation of evidence
failure_modes: Confrontation is anticlimactic — the guilty party simply confesses
The investigator is not in danger during the confrontation
The truth is revealed by accident rather than by the investigator's action

nodes.label: New Understanding
nodes.definition: The mystery is solved and the world is seen clearly — often for the first time. The resolution may bring justice, but it always brings knowledge. The surface order is replaced by a deeper, more honest understanding.
entry_conditions: The confrontation has resolved the mystery
exit_conditions: A new equilibrium is established based on truth rather than concealment
typical_variants: Justice is served and order is restored
Truth is known but justice is incomplete
The investigator is changed by what they learned
The world is darker but more honest
failure_modes: Resolution is too neat — all questions are perfectly answered
Resolution ignores the moral or emotional cost of the investigation
The truth doesn't matter — nothing changes because of it
signals_in_text: The investigator alone with their knowledge

edges.label: Question Emerges
edges.preconditions: Surface order is in place; an event reveals that something is hidden
edges.effects_on_stakes: The stable world is shown to be built on something concealed
edges.effects_on_character: The investigator becomes driven by the need to know
edges.common_alternatives: Question is posed by a crime; question is posed by a personal anomaly
edges.anti_patterns: The question is rhetorical — no one actually needs or wants to investigate

edges.label: Gathers First Clues
edges.preconditions: The investigation has begun; evidence is accessible
edges.effects_on_stakes: The mystery begins to take shape — but incompletely
edges.effects_on_character: The investigator forms initial judgments that will be tested
edges.common_alternatives: Clues come easily; clues require effort and risk to obtain
edges.anti_patterns: First clues solve the mystery entirely

edges.label: Follows the Wrong Lead
edges.preconditions: Initial theory is formed; evidence appears to support it
edges.effects_on_stakes: Time and credibility are spent on a dead end
edges.effects_on_character: The investigator's confidence is tested; humility is forced
edges.common_alternatives: False trail is planted by the culprit; false trail is the investigator's own bias
edges.anti_patterns: False trail is obviously wrong — no reasonable person would follow it

edges.label: Reorients the Investigation
edges.preconditions: The false trail is exposed; new evidence redirects the search
edges.effects_on_stakes: The mystery is bigger or more dangerous than initially thought
edges.effects_on_character: The investigator must reconsider assumptions and face greater risk
edges.common_alternatives: Reorientation comes from a new piece of evidence; from a re-examination of old evidence
edges.anti_patterns: Reorientation is arbitrary — no logical connection to the false trail's failure

edges.label: Connects the Dots
edges.preconditions: Enough evidence has accumulated; a new perspective or clue provides the key
edges.effects_on_stakes: The truth is within reach but acting on it is dangerous
edges.effects_on_character: The investigator's persistence is validated; they must now choose to act
edges.common_alternatives: Breakthrough is a flash of insight; breakthrough is methodical assembly of evidence
edges.anti_patterns: Breakthrough falls into the investigator's lap with no earned effort

edges.label: Forces the Truth Into the Open
edges.preconditions: The truth is known; the investigator must act to reveal or resolve it
edges.effects_on_stakes: Maximum danger — the culprit or system fights back
edges.effects_on_character: The investigator risks everything for the truth
edges.common_alternatives: Confrontation is public; confrontation is private; confrontation is violent
edges.anti_patterns: Confrontation is safe — no risk to the investigator

edges.label: Truth Settles
edges.preconditions: The confrontation has resolved the mystery; the truth is known
edges.effects_on_stakes: External stakes dissolve; the world is seen clearly
edges.effects_on_character: The investigator carries the knowledge — sometimes at personal cost
edges.common_alternatives: Resolution brings justice; resolution brings only knowledge; resolution is ambiguous
edges.anti_patterns: Resolution ignores loose ends; resolution pretends the investigation had no cost
